creator: jhein@symmetricom.com
text:
Never mind about the patch.  I was thinking of a different bug where the
autoloads file was not getting completely built in 8.0.12.

In your case, did you update the vm port after using vm 7.19?  This is
a difference in the recommended setup from the old vm 7.x series.

See also the discussion in https://bugs.launchpad.net/vm/+bug/531969
That thread has a workaround that has been checked in on the 8.1.x
branch.

From INSTALL ...
=============
      GNU Emacs users, have to load the autoloads by hand by adding

         (require 'vm-autoloads)
=============

Add that line to the top of your ~/.vm

Note that the FreeBSD port does not install the 'INSTALL'
file, but you can see it in the work dir if you 'make extract'.

I could add a pkg-message to give a heads up to users updating
from 7.x, but since 8.1.x has a fix and I plan to update to
that soon, I may just do that rather than use pkg-message
or patch the 8.0.14 port.
